title: requests — HTTP routes
description: Two route groups. Tenant-plane routes handle the whole submit → review → decide loop on the caller's tenant. Platform-admin routes are read-only cross-tenant compliance surfaces — approve/reject flow the tenant plane, never the platform plane.

groups.description: Tenant-plane submit + review + decide surface. Every route is scoped to the caller's active tenant + application by the middleware stack. Rate limits per (caller, day) enforced by the framework throttle.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.store
routes.description: Submits a new access request. Body: `resource_type`, `resource_id`, `permissions_wanted[]`, `reason`. Dispatches `CreateAccessRequestAction`, which triggers the `approve` middleware from workflow/approvals and creates the approval_instance under the hood. Returns 202 Accepted because the approval flow is asynchronous — the request is now pending an approver's decision.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.index
routes.description: Lists the caller's own access requests. Default filter: `requester_id=caller.id`. Scoped by tenant + application via middleware.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.show
routes.description: Show a single access request. `id` is the underlying approval_instance.id. Response includes approvers[], decisions[] projected via AccessRequestPresenter.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.withdraw
routes.description: Requester withdraws their own pending request. Delegates to `workflow/approvals::withdraw` — sets `approval_instance.status='cancelled'`. Fires AccessRequestCancelled. Refused on terminal states.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.pending-my-review
routes.description: Lists access requests where the caller is in the resolved approvers set for at least one pending requirement. Backed by a JSONB containment query on `approval_requirements.resolved_approvers`.

queryParams.name: filter[urgency]
queryParams.description: 'expiring_soon' filters to instances with expires_at within 24 hours.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.approve
routes.description: Approver decision. Body: `comment` (optional, max 1000). Delegates to `workflow/approvals::approve` which records the approval_decision and evaluates quorum. If final approval fires ApprovalExecuted → AccessRequestGrantMaterializer kicks in.

routes.name: tenant.access-requests.reject
routes.description: Approver decision. Body: `comment` (required for rejects, max 1000, min 10 — SOC 2 auditable rationale). Sets requirement.status='failed' → instance.status='rejected'.

groups.description: Cross-tenant read-only compliance surface for Academorix ops. No decision endpoints — approve/reject stay on the tenant plane.

routes.name: platform.access-requests.audit-report
routes.description: SOC 2 CC6.1 compliance report. Emits per-tenant metrics: total_requests, approved_count, rejected_count, expired_count, approval_rate, average_time_to_decision_hours, median_time_to_decision_hours.

notes: Every tenant-plane route requires `X-Application-Id` — enforced by `resolve.application` (see hierarchy.md §4). Missing/unknown/disabled headers 400/404/403 per the platform contract.

The `approve` middleware from workflow/approvals is NOT applied at the group level here — only the `POST /access-requests` action carries `#[AsApprovableAction]` and triggers it. The other routes work directly against the already-existing approval_instance.
No public host — access requests are strictly authenticated. Compare to invitations, whose central host serves the invitee token flow.
